Your work will play a critical role in our business offering, your code will deliver value to our customers faster than ever before, and your attention to detail and commitment to quality will be critical in ensuring the success of our products.
   

  

 Using design documentation and functional programming specifications, you will be responsible for implementing identified components. You will ensure that implemented components are appropriately documented, unit\\-tested, and ready for integration into the final product. You will have the opportunity to architect the solution, test the code, and deploy and build a CI/CD pipeline for it.
   

  

 As a valued member of our team, you will provide work estimates for assigned development work, and guide features, functional objectives, or technologies being built for interested parties. Your contributions will have a significant impact on our products' success, and you will be part of a team that is passionate about innovation, creativity, and excellence. Above all else, you will have the freedom to drive innovation and take ownership of your work while honing your problem\\-solving, collaboration, and automation skills. Together, we can make a difference in the world of cloud\\-based managed services.
   

  

 Your Future at Kyndryl
   

  

 The career path ahead is full of exciting opportunities to grow and advance within the job family. With dedication and hard work, you can climb the ladder to higher bands, achieving coveted positions such as Principal Engineer or Vice President of Software. These roles not only offer the chance to inspire and innovate, but also bring with them a sense of pride and accomplishment for having reached the pinnacle of your career in the software industry.
   

  

**Who You Are**
 You’re good at what you do and possess the required experience to prove it. However, equally as important – you have a growth mindset; keen to drive your own personal and professional development. You are customer\\-focused – someone who prioritizes customer success in their work. And finally, you’re open and borderless – naturally inclusive in how you work with others.
   

  

**Required Technical And Professional Experience**
* 6\\+ years of experience working as a software engineer on complex software projects
* Excellent coding skills and solid development experience (Java, SpringBoot) with debugging and problem\\-solving skills
* AWS development experience
* Experience with AWS Lambda, AWS services, and microservices.
* Software development methodologies, with demonstrated experience developing scalable and robust software
* Experienced in relational and NoSQL databases, data mapping, XML/JSON, Rest based web services
* Knowledge of architecture design \\- Microservices architecture, containers (Docker \\& k8s), messaging queues
* Deep understanding of OOP and Design patterns


**Preferred Technical And Professional Experience**
* Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, related technical field, or equivalent practical experience
* Certification in one or more of the hyperscalers (Azure, AWS, and Google GCP) \\- otherwise, you can obtain certifications with Kyndryl
* Experience with DevOps tools and modern engineering practices


**Being You**
 Diversity is a whole lot more than what we look like or where we come from, it’s how we think and who we are. We welcome people of all cultures, backgrounds, and experiences. But we’re not doing it single\\-handily: Our Kyndryl Inclusion Networks are only one of many ways we create a workplace where all Kyndryls can find and provide support and advice. This dedication to welcoming everyone into our company means that Kyndryl gives you – and everyone next to you – the ability to bring your whole self to work, individually and collectively, and support the activation of our equitable culture. That’s the Kyndryl Way.
   

  

**What You Can Expect**
 With state\\-of\\-the\\-art resources and Fortune 100 clients, every day is an opportunity to innovate, build new capabilities, new relationships, new processes, and new value. Kyndryl cares about your well\\-being and prides itself on offering benefits that give you choice, reflect the diversity of our employees and support you and your family through the moments that matter – wherever you are in your life journey. Our employee learning programs give you access to the best learning in the industry to receive certifications, including Microsoft, Google, Amazon, Skillsoft, and many more. Through our company\\-wide volunteering and giving platform, you can donate, start fundraisers, volunteer, and search over 2 million non\\-profit organizations. At Kyndryl, we invest heavily in you, we want you to succeed so that together, we will all succeed.
   

  

**Get Referred!**

title: ITS System Admin/Engr II (CIS), Corporate Infrastructure Product Management
description:
**This job is with Amazon, an inclusive employer and a member of myGwork – the largest global platform for the LGBTQ\\+ business community. Please do not contact the recruiter directly.**
**Description**
 We are looking for a Systems Engineer to join the CIPM AUX Engineering team and own the technical execution layer of our power infrastructure lifecycle program. This is an embedded engineering role within a product management team; you will be the technical hands and eyes of the auxiliary power space, working alongside the Technical Infrastructure Program Manager (TIPM) who coordinates and owns the overall program.
   

  

 You will lead lab testing and validation of UPS systems, PDUs, and energy management platforms; execute and support pilot deployments at corporate office sites; create and maintain deployment runbooks, firmware control documentation, and RMA processes; and track the health, compliance, and configuration integrity of the production AUX fleet. You will consult on power requirements for Core Network and Compute hardware, evaluate new auxiliary platforms and management software, and ensure that all products meet CIS standards before handover to delivery teams.
   

  

 This role is ideal for an engineer who thrives at the intersection of hands\\-on power infrastructure work and structured program execution, and who wants to grow their impact in a team that directly shapes energy management strategy at Amazon scale.
   

  

 Key job responsibilities
   

  

 Fleet Lifecycle \\& Health Supervision
   

  

* Review quarterly all production Golden Configs (GC)
* Review quarterly firmware status for AUX fleet
* Review quarterly product catalog and runbooks
* Warranty monitoring and troubleshooting
* Coordinate firmware patching schedule with site teams
* Provide Tier\\-3 on\\-call support for technically complex AUX issues
* Manage ticket queue of technical product issues or documentation updates
* Log defects in tracker; warranty deployments to confirm process is self\\-service


 Lab Testing \\& Pilot Execution
   

  

* Lead lab proof of concept testing for new UPS/PDU platforms and management software
* Review new product pilot requirements
* Pilot site execution \\- shadow and reverse shadow deployments
* Conduct training sessions for OID on AUX deployment procedures
* Shadow/reverse shadow knowledge transfer for new UPS/PDU products
* Create and deliver Lunch \\& Learn sessions for new auxiliary device standards
* Adhere to CIS standards (develop runbook, coordinate initial integration with SBA and internal tooling)
* Review lab changes, track AUX inventory, propose infrastructure changes to support testing
* Track proof of concept hardware (AUX, Core, Compute) installation and testing requirements


 Technical Documentation
   

  

* Build deployment runbooks (create, validate, iterate)
* Build migration/LCR runbooks (create, validate, iterate)
* Build maintenance and service tracking runbook
* Update monitoring runbook for new products
* Update firmware control document for CVE remediation
* Develop RMA process for auxiliary devices
* Define, test, and release password management process
* Maintain comprehensive tracking of configuration changes within corporate asset management and EMS platforms
* Maintain comprehensive process to track maintenance and incidents
* Test and implement process improvements or technology upgrades to enhance power reliability and operational efficiency


 Technical Design \\& Standards Ownership
   

  

* Define and validate technical designs for compute infrastructure deployments
* Validate Bill of Materials (BoM) specifications against site and service requirements
* Help defining hardware configuration tiers to serve multiple customer profiles and budget constraints
* Support vendor evaluation and platform certification efforts, including technical validation of alternative compute platforms


 Automation \\& Tooling
   

  

* Test, review, and approve automation processes for AUX infrastructure
* Automation review and testing for deployment and maintenance workflows


 Post\\-Deployment Supervision \\& Knowledge Transfer
   

  

* Warranty monitoring, troubleshooting, and validation
* On\\-call for Tier\\-3 issues
* Manage ticket queue of technical product issues or documentation updates
* Coordinate firmware patching schedule with site teams
* Warranty deployments to confirm process is self\\-service
* Log defects in tracker


**About The Team**
 Corporate Infrastructure Product Management (CIPM) is responsible for the hardware fleet lifecycle of Amazon's corporate infrastructure, encompassing vendor and platform strategy, firmware and patching compliance, fleet inventory and lifecycle management, and hardware capacity planning. CIPM serves as both product owner and strategic driver of the infrastructure hardware roadmap, collaborating closely with partner engineering and deployment teams across the organization.
   

  

 Within CIPM, the Auxiliary (AUX) Engineering function owns the power distribution and energy management layer that underpins all corporate office infrastructure. The team manages a global fleet of auxiliary power hardware \\- including UPS systems, PDUs, and environment sensors \\- across corporate office sites worldwide. This fleet provides reliable, efficient power distribution and power supply to every CIS\\-hosted product deployed in corporate offices, covering new builds, lifecycle replacements (LCR), expansions, maintenance, and proof of concept programs. The team onboards new auxiliary products through a repeatable process and validates them against CIS standards and site\\-specific requirements, ensuring consistent power reliability at Amazon scale.
   

  

**Basic Qualifications**
* 5\\+ years of systems engineering experience
* Experience designing and developing scripts to automate operational burdens and reviewing scripting changes to ensure they meet the standards for maintainability, scalability and security
* Experience in data center, networking engineering, systems engineering or construction management with a good understanding of data center technologies such as power, cooling and controls systems
* Experience with power management and power monitoring systems
* Experience with enterprise UPS/PDU platforms from major manufacturers (APC/Schneider Electric, Eaton, Vertiv, or equivalent)


**Preferred Qualifications**
* Knowledge of TCP/IP and networking protocols such as HTTP and DNS
* Experience working in 24/7 production environment
* Experience with service\\-oriented architecture and web services
* Exposure to lifecycle replacement programs or hardware end\\-of\\-life/end\\-of\\-support planning
* Experience developing or managing RMA processes and warranty tracking for hardware fleets


 Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\\-we\\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you're applying in isn't listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.]]\\>

**Job Summary**
 We are looking for a senior process engineer with extensive experience in either Energy sector (Refining, LNG, Petrochemicals) or Pharmaceutical/Data Center/Semiconductor sector. The engineer would be required to utilize their expansive knowledge in all areas of design from conceptual studies to detailed engineering. The engineer should have a proven track record and will be recognized for their understanding of the key process concepts. They have expertise in standard process deliverables such as design basis, H\\&MB, PFDs, process descriptions, P\\&IDs, etc and would be comfortable both originating as well as approving these documents. Simulation experience is highly desirable and experience in multiple software (Hysys, Pro II, Unisim, Promax) will be an advantage. Candidate should have worked in project teams carrying out basic and detailed engineering for execution of projects on various modes of execution. Senior Engineer will utilize Bechtel procedures and work processes along with sound Process understanding of Process design principles to develop project deliverables. The engineer will drive for quality of deliverables and minimize rework through the application of quality absolutes while working independently / leading a team or as part of a team.
   

  

 This position is based in Chennai, India
   

  

* Executes process deliverables according to a set engineering design criterion utilizing approved engineering procedures, practices, and codes and standards.
* Takes the initiative to coordinate the design development with other engineering disciplines while managing workload and meeting schedule due dates.
* Knowledgeable of and performs standard discipline engineering activities on projects with minimal technical oversight.

* Uses specialized engineering hydraulic programs such as Flarenet, Pipeflo, PIPENET, VMGSim, etc to interpret or complete calculations.

**Major Responsibilities**

* Perform design calculations using standard simulation programs (Hysys, Pro II, Unisim, and/or Promax).
* Conversant in relief calculation methodologies and industry codes and standards applicable to pressure relief design. Develop relief load narratives, relief load calculations and relief valve datasheets.
